assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md states '## Classification: code change request' and 'A sub-task will be created to address this feedback.' The report table shows comment 30001 classified as 'code change request' with action 'Sub-task created'. The file subtask-30001.md exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states '## Classification: suggestion' and the Reasoning section explicitly identifies suggestive language: 'The reviewer uses suggestive language: "should also add", "would help", "Something like". The phrase "should also" proposes an addition beyond the current scope rather than requesting a correction to existing code. The phrase "would help" indicates an optional performance improvement, not a required fix.'

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md states '## Classification: nit' and 'No sub-task created.' The report table shows comment 30003 classified as 'nit' with action 'No sub-task (minor style feedback)'. No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md states '## Classification: question' and 'No sub-task created.' The report table shows comment 30004 classified as 'question' with action 'No sub-task (clarification request; behavior is intentional per task description)'. No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1, value 'trustify-backend'), '## Description' (line 3), '## Files to Modify' (line 7), '## Implementation Notes' (line 10), and '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 20). Only one sub-task was created (subtask-30001.md), and it satisfies all template requirements.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' section (line 29) followed by the exact URL 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 30).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' section (line 32) with Comment ID 30001, Author reviewer-a, File reference, and the full original comment text: 'The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction. If the sbom_advisory update fails after sbom_package succeeds, you\'ll have inconsistent state. Wrap the three operations in `self.db.transaction(|txn| { ... })` and use `txn` instead of `self.db` for each exec call.'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only markdown files: report.md, review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md, and subtask-30001.md. No code files (.rs, .toml, .py, etc.) exist in the outputs directory. All output files are classification reports, sub-task descriptions, or the verification report.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have corresponding classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report's Review Feedback Details table lists all four comments with classifications and actions. The report summary states '4 comments classified: 1 code change request (sub-task created), 1 suggestion, 1 nit, 1 question'.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md file contains no mention of merging, auto-merging, or merge commands. The overall status is 'WARN' indicating issues need attention. The report only creates sub-tasks and provides verification findings -- no merge action is taken or suggested.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: The report contains '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file with 5 new test functions; no existing test files were modified or deleted |'. The Test Change Classification Details section further states: 'Classification: ADDITIVE' and 'tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file (not present on base branch) containing 5 new test functions and 7+ assertions. No existing test files were modified or deleted. All test changes are purely additive.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a detailed 'Convention Upgrade Eligibility' section that evaluates three criteria: (1) CONVENTIONS.md check -- 'does not document index creation patterns for migration files', criterion not met; (2) Codebase pattern check -- 'the diff does not contain evidence of a consistent codebase pattern', criterion not met; (3) Performance-related scrutiny -- 'the upgrade decision requires project-specific evidence', general best practices excluded. Conclusion: 'The suggestion does not qualify for upgrade to code change request because neither CONVENTIONS.md nor counted codebase patterns support it.' The report table also notes for comment 30002: 'No sub-task (not backed by project convention)'.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md classification is 'suggestion' with conclusion 'It remains classified as a suggestion. No sub-task created.' No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. The report table shows comment 30002 with action 'No sub-task (not backed by project convention)'. The convention upgrade evaluation found no CONVENTIONS.md documentation or codebase patterns to support upgrading.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: The report's Test Quality row states: 'Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews found on this PR)'. The Test Quality Details section confirms: 'Eval Quality: N/A -- No eval result reviews detected on this PR.' The overall Test Quality result is 'PASS' showing Eval Quality did not negatively affect the combination. While the report says 'N/A' and 'no eval result reviews detected/found', it does not explicitly mention the 3-criteria detection method (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals). However, the result is correct: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist.
